title: bromus asper subsp. serotinus
intro: Bromus asper subsp. serotinus, also known as the autumn soft brome, is a subspecies of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Bromus asper subsp. serotinus include its ability to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions made from its leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in ointments and salves.
